title: buchanania lonchophylla
intro: Buchanania lonchophylla, also known as the Manan or Uvada, is a plant species belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an antiseptic agent. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and pain, as well as providing antioxidant properties. Buchanania lonchophylla can be prepared into herbal remedies like infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
